entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The 3UR-FE 5.7L V8 brought 381hp and 544Nm to the Sequoia, becoming the definitive US import engine for UK enthusiasts seeking towing power and reliability.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (metallic ticking on cold start, 80k-120k miles)
Cam tower oil leak (valley seals fail, often misdiagnosed as head gasket)
Heat exchanger/valley plate coolant leak (causes corrosion in engine V)

entries.title: 4.6L Petrol
entries.history: The transitional 1UR-FE 4.6L V8 offered 314hp between 2010-2012, bridging first and second generation Sequoias with improved fuel economy.
commonFailures: Valve spring breakage (early production units, catastrophic if missed)
Secondary air injection system failure (CEL, emissions-related)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1UR-FE Toyota Sequoia engine replacement

entries.title: 4.7L Petrol
entries.history: The robust 2UZ-FE 4.7L V8 powered first-generation Sequoias with proven reliability, though its interference design demands strict timing belt maintenance.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference engine - catastrophic if belt snaps)
Exhaust manifold cracks (ticking noise on cold start)
Starter motor location (under intake - labour-intensive replacement)

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Sequoia Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Sequoia's V8 engines are robust but not immune to age-related failures. The 3UR-FE 5.7L dominates UK replacement demand due to timing chain tensioner issues and cam tower leaks, while the 2UZ-FE 4.7L requires strict timing belt adherence. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why 3UR-FE Engines Develop Metallic Ticking
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner sticks when cold, allowing chain slap and accelerated wear on guides and sprockets.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, inspect chain/guides, reset codes
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before guide damage; may not resolve if wear already advanced

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3UR-FE with upgraded tensioner components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; recommended for high-mileage units or if chain damage confirmed

problems.recommendation: If your Sequoia has under 100k miles and the ticking is recent, a specialist tensioner replacement may suffice. However, for engines over 120k miles or with confirmed guide wear, a rebuilt 3UR-FE offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3UR-FE engine prices for your Toyota Sequoia

problems.h4: Cam Tower Oil Leak - The Valley Seal Failure Mistaken for Head Gasket
problems.rootCause: Seals between cam carriers and cylinder head degrade, allowing oil to seep into the engine valley and burn on hot exhaust components.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake, replace cam carrier seals, reassemble with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive; viable if engine otherwise healthy and under 120k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3UR-FE with updated sealing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if leak is severe, engine has high mileage, or other issues present

problems.recommendation: Cam tower resealing is complex and costly due to intake removal. For Sequoias over 120k miles or with multiple minor issues, investing in a rebuilt engine often provides better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 3UR-FE cam tower leak repair or replacement

problems.h4: Heat Exchanger Coolant Leak - Valley Plate Failure Causing Corrosion
problems.rootCause: Valley plate sealer fails, allowing coolant to leak into the V of the engine, leading to internal corrosion and potential head gasket compromise.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valley plate, reseal heat exchanger, flush cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before corrosion spreads; requires thorough inspection

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3UR-FE with updated sealing, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if corrosion is confirmed or engine shows signs of overheating damage

problems.recommendation: Coolant leaks in the valley are serious. If inspection reveals corrosion or the engine has exceeded 130k miles, replacement with a rebuilt unit is the safer, more economical long-term choice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3UR-FE engines with warranty for your Sequoia

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure Risk - The 2UZ-FE Interference Engine Warning
problems.rootCause: The 2UZ-FE is an interference engine; if the timing belt snaps, pistons strike valves causing catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump, seals per manufacturer interval
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on schedule

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2UZ-FE with updated components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if belt has failed; also recommended for high-mileage engines with unknown service history

problems.recommendation: If your 2UZ-FE Sequoia has a documented timing belt history and is under 100k miles, preventative service is cost-effective. For engines with unknown history or over 120k miles, a rebuilt unit offers greater reliability and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 2UZ-FE timing belt service or engine replacement

engineYears.h2: Toyota Sequoia Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Sequoia's production timeline, organised so UK import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from specialists.

years.description: The first-generation Sequoia launched with the proven 2UZ-FE 4.7L V8, establishing the platform's reputation for towing and reliability among UK import enthusiasts.
keyChanges: 2004: Minor facelift with updated grille and interior trim
2005: Introduction of optional towing package with upgraded cooling
2007: Final model year before second-generation redesign
knownFor: Robust 2UZ-FE engine when timing belt replaced every 90k miles
Exhaust manifold cracks causing cold-start ticking noise
Starter motor location under intake makes replacement labour-intensive
Strong residual values among UK import community for towing applications
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2007 Toyota Sequoia replacement engines
years.ticker: Sequoia 2001 4.7 engine replacement • 2UZ-FE timing belt failure UK • Sequoia first generation engine cost

years.description: The all-new second-generation Sequoia debuted with the powerful 3UR-FE 5.7L V8, delivering 381hp and transforming towing capability for UK import owners.
keyChanges: Introduction of Platinum trim level with premium features
Improved towing capacity up to 7,400 lbs with proper equipment
New chassis with independent rear suspension for better ride quality
knownFor: Significant power increase over first generation (381hp vs 280hp)
Early reports of timing chain tensioner sticking on cold start
Cam tower seal design prone to oil leaks into engine valley
High demand among UK import specialists for towing and overlanding
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Toyota Sequoia replacement engines
years.ticker: Sequoia 2008 5.7 engine replacement • 3UR-FE timing chain noise UK • Sequoia second generation engine cost

years.description: Select markets received the 1UR-FE 4.6L V8 as a fuel-economy alternative to the 5.7L, though UK imports predominantly feature the more powerful 3UR-FE.
keyChanges: 2010: Introduction of 1UR-FE 4.6L V8 option (314hp) for SR5 and Limited trims
Minor interior updates and technology enhancements
2012: Last year for 1UR-FE availability in Sequoia lineup
knownFor: 1UR-FE offered better fuel economy but lower towing capacity than 3UR-FE
Early 1UR-FE units (2010) had valve spring breakage concerns
3UR-FE remained the preferred choice for UK import towing applications
Extended cranking issues reported on cold starts (P1604/P1605 codes)
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Toyota Sequoia replacement engines

years.description: The Sequoia received cosmetic updates and minor refinements while the 3UR-FE 5.7L remained the sole engine option for UK imports, with known failure patterns becoming well-documented.
keyChanges: 2013: Facelift with updated front fascia and interior technology
Introduction of Toyota Entune infotainment system
Enhanced safety features including blind spot monitoring (Platinum)
Continued production with 3UR-FE 5.7L as standard engine
knownFor: Timing chain tensioner failure pattern well-established by this period
Cam tower and heat exchanger leaks driving replacement enquiries
Strong demand for rebuilt 3UR-FE engines among UK import owners
TRD Pro trim introduced for off-road enthusiasts (2015)
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2017 Toyota Sequoia replacement engines
years.ticker: Sequoia 2015 5.7 engine replacement • 3UR-FE cam tower leak UK • Sequoia TRD Pro engine cost

years.description: The Sequoia received its final major update in 2018 with styling and technology refreshes, while the dependable 3UR-FE 5.7L V8 continued unchanged through end of production.
keyChanges: 2018: Final facelift with new grille, LED lighting, updated interior
Introduction of Toyota Safety Sense P suite (Platinum standard)
Enhanced towing technology and trailer backup assist
2022: Final model year before Sequoia redesign on TNGA-F platform
mainEngines: 3UR-FE - 5.7L petrol V8 (SR5 / Limited / Platinum / TRD Pro)
knownFor: 3UR-FE reliability improved with updated tensioner designs post-2015
Cam tower and heat exchanger leaks remain primary replacement drivers
High residual values among UK import community for late-model units
Final production year (2022) models command premium in used import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2022 Toyota Sequoia replacement engines
years.ticker: Sequoia 2021 5.7 engine replacement • 3UR-FE heat exchanger leak UK • Sequoia final year engine cost

faq.h2: Toyota Sequoia Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Sequoia engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a Toyota Sequoia engine in the UK typically costs £1,500-£7,000 depending on engine code, condition and whether you choose used, reconditioned or rebuilt. The 2UZ-FE 4.7L ranges £1,500-£3,000, while the 3UR-FE 5.7L ranges £3,500-£6,000 for rebuilt units supply-only.
keyPoints: 2UZ-FE 4.7L rebuilt: £1,500-£3,000 supply-only
3UR-FE 5.7L rebuilt: £3,500-£6,000 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £800-£2,500 depending on specialist
All EngineMarket suppliers include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Toyota Sequoia engine prices now

items.question: Will a Tundra engine fit my Toyota Sequoia?
items.answer: Yes, the 3UR-FE 5.7L and 2UZ-FE 4.7L engines are shared between the Toyota Sequoia and Tundra, making cross-platform swaps possible with minor ancillary adjustments for UK import owners.
keyPoints: 3UR-FE 5.7L fits both Sequoia and Tundra (2008-2022)
2UZ-FE 4.7L fits both Sequoia and Tundra (2001-2007)
Minor differences in mounts or wiring may require adaptation
Always verify engine code matches your Sequoia's original specification

items.question: What is the most reliable Toyota Sequoia engine?
items.answer: The 2UZ-FE 4.7L is widely regarded as the most reliable Sequoia engine when timing belt maintenance is strictly followed, though the 3UR-FE 5.7L offers superior power and towing capability for UK import owners.
keyPoints: 2UZ-FE: Proven reliability with strict timing belt intervals
3UR-FE: More power but known for tensioner and seal issues
Both engines can exceed 200k miles with proper maintenance
Rebuilt units with upgraded components offer best long-term value
items.cta: Compare reliability ratings for Sequoia engines

items.question: What causes the ticking noise on cold start in my Sequoia 5.7L?
items.answer: A metallic ticking noise on cold start in the Sequoia 5.7L (3UR-FE) is typically caused by timing chain tensioner failure, where the hydraulic tensioner sticks when cold, allowing chain slap against guides.
keyPoints: Ticking indicates tensioner not maintaining chain tension when cold
Ignoring it can lead to guide wear and catastrophic chain failure
Repair cost £500-£1,500; replacement engine £3,500-£6,000
For engines over 120k miles, replacement often better value
items.cta: Get quotes for 3UR-FE timing chain repair or replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing my Toyota Sequoia engine or should I sell?
items.answer: Replacing your Sequoia engine is usually worth it if the vehicle is otherwise in good condition, has low body/chassis corrosion, and you plan to keep it long-term for towing or overlanding use in the UK.
keyPoints: Replacement cost £1,500-£6,000 vs vehicle value £5,000-£25,000
Rebuilt engine with warranty extends life 100k+ miles typically
Selling a Sequoia with engine issues yields minimal return
Import specialists can advise on cost-benefit for your specific case
items.cta: Calculate Sequoia repair vs replace value with our tool

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement engine for my Toyota Sequoia in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who source and supply Toyota Sequoia engines, including US import experts familiar with 3UR-FE, 2UZ-FE and 1UR-FE compatibility and documentation requirements.
keyPoints: EngineMarket network includes US import V8 specialists
All suppliers provide warranty (minimum 12 months rebuilt)
Compare multiple quotes instantly via our free comparison tool
Nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available
items.cta: Get free quotes from UK Toyota Sequoia engine specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Toyota Sequoia engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Toyota Sequoia engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with some suppliers offering extended coverage for an additional fee.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units
Unlimited mileage coverage typical for specialist rebuilds
Used engines may have shorter warranty (6 months typical)
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Sequoia engine suppliers

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Toyota Sequoia engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a Toyota Sequoia engine typically takes 2-4 days at a specialist UK garage familiar with US import V8s, plus any additional time for engine sourcing and delivery from supplier to workshop.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 1-3 days depending on stock availability
Removal and installation: 2-3 days at specialist workshop
ECU adaptation and road testing: 0.5-1 day additional
Total timeline: 3-7 days from order to collection typical
items.cta: Check turnaround times for Sequoia engine supply & fit

items.question: Can I fit a used US-spec engine to my UK-imported Toyota Sequoia?
items.answer: Yes, US-spec engines can be fitted to UK-imported Sequoias provided the engine code matches your vehicle's original specification and all ancillary components are compatible with your model's wiring and emissions setup.
keyPoints: Engine code must match original (3UR-FE, 2UZ-FE, or 1UR-FE)
Verify ancillary compatibility (alternator, AC, emissions)
DVLA may require documentation for engine change on import
Specialist import garages can handle adaptation and certification
items.cta: Verify US engine compatibility for your UK Sequoia

items.question: Where is the engine code located on my Toyota Sequoia?
items.answer: The engine code on a Toyota Sequoia is stamped on the engine block near the front, visible from the driver's side wheel arch, and also listed on the vehicle's VIN plate and import documentation for verification.
keyPoints: Stamped on block near front, driver's side wheel arch
Also on VIN plate under bonnet and import paperwork
Codes: 3UR-FE (5.7L), 2UZ-FE (4.7L), 1UR-FE (4.6L)
Always verify code before ordering replacement engine
items.cta: Identify your Sequoia engine code with our lookup tool
